Source_Species.reason: The paper states the four bioluminescence genes, including nnluz, are from Neonothopanus nambi.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Neonothopanus nambi is explicitly named as the source of nnluz.

Applied_Species.reason: Transgenic tobacco plants were engineered and tested; tobacco refers to Nicotiana tabacum in the paper.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The main engineered plants are explicitly Nicotiana tabacum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify Nicotiana tabacum cv. Petit Havana SR1 for transformation.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state each gene was cloned under the constitutive 35S promoter from cauliflower mosaic virus.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The direct output measured for the transgene set including nnluz is light emission/bioluminescence.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results report luminescence across the whole plant, brightest in flowers, terminal/axillary buds and young shoots, root branching points, and increased at injury sites.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: nnluz is explicitly identified as luciferase; this supports fungal luciferase.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper shows that plants expressing nnluz with the other fungal genes exhibit autonomous visible luminescence without exogenous substrate.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three elements are supported: nnluz was part of the four-gene integration set in N. tabacum and a similar glowing N. benthamiana strain; luminescence occurred at all developmental stages and was brightest in flowers; qPCR was performed to test whether nnluz expression oscillates during the day.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Methods/results support stable Agrobacterium transformation, PCR confirmation, Southern blot, luminescence imaging, and qPCR for nnluz transcript analysis.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results describe altered luminescence after leaf injury, apical shoot removal, methyl jasmonate, ripe banana skin exposure, and across developmental stages.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Because nnluz was placed under the constitutive 35S promoter, constitutive transgene expression is supported by construct design.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results state no differences in phenotype, chlorophyll/carotenoid content, flowering time, and seed germination, except a 12% increase in median height.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The study uses nnluz as part of a fungal bioluminescence reporter pathway, and precursor experiments suggest light output depends on caffeic acid availability.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper explicitly frames autonomous luminescence as a tool for monitoring dynamic plant processes non-invasively.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: nnluz was co-expressed in the introduced pathway with nnhisps, nnh3h, and nncph; functional interaction within the pathway is directly described.

Source_Species.reason: nncph is listed among the four bioluminescence genes from Neonothopanus nambi.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Neonothopanus nambi is explicitly given as the source species.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The main transformed species was Nicotiana tabacum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods identify Nicotiana tabacum cv. Petit Havana SR1.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state each gene was under the constitutive CaMV 35S promoter.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The measured output of the four-gene system including nncph is plant bioluminescence.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Luminescence is reported throughout the plant, so whole plant is supported.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Figure 1 legend defines CPH as putative caffeoyl pyruvate hydrolase.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: nncph is included as one of the four genes of the fungal caffeic acid cycle used to generate autonomous luminescence in plants.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed points are supported: nncph was one of four genes assembled; it is included as CPH in the caffeic acid cycle; methods describe a cph-less construct whereas the main luminous plants used the four-gene construct.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Stable transformation and the luminescence phenotype of the four-gene plants are supported, but the paper does not directly validate nncph individually.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Constitutive expression is supported by the 35S promoter construct design.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper reports no obvious burden or toxicity for the four-gene luminescent system, aside from increased median height.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The label 'assigned as a common gene because it functions in recycling/hydrolase support within the luminescent cycle and was not the focal nutrient-biosynthetic bottleneck analyzed in depth' is an interpretive classification not stated in the paper.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: nncph functions in the same introduced pathway with nnluz, nnhisps, and nnh3h, which is supported by the construct and pathway figure.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The study presents luminescence as an autonomous reporting tool.

corrections.old_value: Assigned as a common gene because it functions in recycling/hydrolase support within the luminescent cycle and was not the focal nutrient-biosynthetic bottleneck analyzed in depth.
corrections.reason: The label 'assigned as a common gene because it functions in recycling/hydrolase support within the luminescent cycle and was not the focal nutrient-biosynthetic bottleneck analyzed in depth' is an interpretive classification not stated in the paper.

Enzyme_Name.reason: nnhisps is explicitly identified as hispidin synthase.

Source_Species.reason: The paper lists nnhisps among genes from Neonothopanus nambi.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The main transformed species was Nicotiana tabacum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify Nicotiana tabacum cv. Petit Havana SR1.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state the gene was placed under the constitutive 35S promoter.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper explicitly describes the fungal bioluminescence system as the caffeic acid cycle integrated into plant metabolism.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It is reasonable that HispS acts in hispidin biosynthesis, but the paper does not use the exact phrase 'hispidin biosynthesis branch'.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: HispS acts between caffeic acid and downstream intermediates, but 'midstream' is an interpretive label not stated explicitly.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper states the fungal system converts caffeic acid into luciferin, and HispS is the enzyme making hispidin in this pathway, supporting that it forms a luciferin precursor from plant-derived caffeic acid.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Precursor infiltration experiments and pathway description support caffeic acid, malonyl-CoA, CoA, and ATP as hispidin precursors for this step.

Primary_Product.reason: The enzyme name hispidin synthase and pathway logic support hispidin as the product.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This is consistent with the pathway in Figure 1b and infiltration experiments testing caffeic acid, malonyl-CoA, CoA, and ATP as hispidin precursors.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper names the enzyme hispidin synthase but does not state 'polyketide synthase-like' in the provided text.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The pathway output measured is bioluminescence.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper frames the luminescent output as a reporter tool.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results describe luminescence throughout the plant, strongest in flowers and young active tissues.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: nnhisps is part of the required introduced pathway, and experiments suggest pathway flux depends on caffeic acid supply feeding hispidin biosynthesis.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly states caffeic acid limits hispidin biosynthesis, while malonyl-CoA, CoA, and ATP did not increase luminescence.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Stable transformation, precursor infiltration assays, and luminescence imaging are all described.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed treatments/conditions are described in the paper as affecting luminescence or used in precursor assays.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Constitutive transgene expression is supported by use of the 35S promoter.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: nnhisps was co-introduced and functions with nnh3h, nnluz, and nncph in the pathway.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These points match the paper: nnhisps is a core enzyme in the introduced pathway; hispidin injection caused immediate bright luminescence; caffeic acid but not malonyl-CoA/CoA/ATP increased luminescence, indicating caffeic acid-limited hispidin biosynthesis.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: No obvious toxicity or major growth penalty was reported for the transgenic plants overall, apart from increased height.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately summarizes the paper's mechanistic conclusion that endogenous caffeic acid links phenylpropanoid metabolism to fungal luciferin production.

corrections.reason: The paper names the enzyme hispidin synthase but does not state 'polyketide synthase-like' in the provided text.

Enzyme_Name.reason: nnh3h is explicitly identified as hispidin-3-hydroxylase.

Source_Species.reason: The gene is listed among those from Neonothopanus nambi.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The main transformed species was Nicotiana tabacum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify the variety Petit Havana SR1.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state each gene was under the constitutive 35S promoter.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper describes this fungal bioluminescence pathway as the caffeic acid cycle.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The placement in luciferin formation is inferable from the pathway, but the exact phrase 'luciferin formation branch' is not used.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: nnh3h acts after hispidin synthase and before luciferase in the pathway figure, so downstream is directly inferable.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Figure 1b places H3H between hispidin and the luciferase substrate, supporting formation of luciferin/immediate light-emitting substrate from hispidin.

Primary_Substrate.reason: As hispidin-3-hydroxylase in the pathway, its substrate is hispidin; this is directly inferable from the enzyme name and pathway figure.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper describes the cycle and mentions luciferin, but the specific product name '3-hydroxyhispidin' does not appear in the provided text.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Hydroxylation of hispidin to luciferin is inferable from the enzyme name and pathway, but the exact reaction description is not explicitly stated in the paper text provided.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The enzyme is explicitly called hispidin-3-hydroxylase, supporting 'hydroxylase'.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The pathway output measured is light emission/bioluminescence.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The luminescent output is presented as an autonomous reporter.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Luminescence is observed throughout the plant.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: nnh3h is one of the four genes used to obtain visible autonomous luminescence, supporting its required role in the pathway.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not identify nnh3h as rate-limiting, so 'not established as rate-limiting' is consistent with the evidence.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Stable transformation, pathway design, luminescence imaging, and inference from precursor experiments are all part of the paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Developmental stage and phenylpropanoid-inducing conditions are discussed as affecting luminescence in the engineered plants.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Constitutive expression is supported by the 35S promoter construct design.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: nnh3h functions in the same introduced pathway as nnhisps, nnluz, and nncph.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The paper supports that nnh3h was one of the four genes sufficient for visible autonomous luminescence, that it is positioned between hispidin formation and luciferase in the pathway, and that the pathway enables endogenous luciferin generation without exogenous substrate.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: No obvious toxicity was reported for the luminescent plants overall.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This matches the paper's presentation of nnh3h as a core pathway enzyme not individually dissected genetically.
